title: Meridian Agent Manifest (domain-team submission)
description: Canonical contract a domain team submits to register one agent. A2A-Agent-Card-aligned with gateway extensions (protocol, connection, constraints). Input schemas are DERIVED by the gateway via introspection. output_schema is an optional fallback only when protocol introspection cannot derive an output schema. Validate submissions against this BEFORE introspection and storage.

domain.description: World B: the business domain this agent belongs to. NOT enumerated here — a new domain onboards by adding its manifests; the gateway validates this against the loaded domain manifests at boot (DomainManifestStore), not against a hardcoded list.

audience.description: Who may reach this agent. 'segment' = gated by business-segment membership + per-segment classification. 'enterprise' = open to all authenticated users (segment gate skipped) — e.g. HR policy Q&A.

protocol.description: Gateway extension: which adapter invokes this agent.

connection.description: How to reach the agent. Required keys depend on protocol (see conditionals below).

examples.description: Representative prompts; embedded for semantic routing. At least 3.

access_mode.description: Phase 1 agents MUST be "read"; the resolver/entitlement layer enforces read-only. Replaces the former boolean is_mutating.

sub_domain.description: World B: sub-domain this agent belongs to within its domain (e.g. private-banking); resolves to a loaded sub-domain manifest.

max_response_tokens.description: World B: gateway truncates this agent's response to this many tokens before synthesis.

output_schema.description: OPTIONAL JSON Schema fallback for the agent's output. Used only when HTTP/MCP introspection cannot derive an output schema; otherwise the gateway stores the introspected schema.

io.description: OPTIONAL semantic dataflow contract for multi-step (DAG) orchestration. This is NOT the wire input/output schema (that is still DERIVED via introspection) — it declares, in domain terms, what this capability NEEDS and what it PRODUCES, so the deterministic DAG resolver can wire dependent steps (producer.output type == downstream consumer.from). Capabilities without 'io' remain single-node (flat fan-out) only; the DAG path lights up per-capability as contracts are declared. World B: 'entity' references a sub-domain entity_types key; produced/consumed 'type' values are manifest-declared symbolic strings the gateway matches by equality — never hardcoded in gateway Java.

consumes.description: Inputs this capability needs. Each item is EITHER an 'entity' (a leaf input, satisfied by deterministic entity resolution) OR a 'from' (an upstream capability's produced output 'type', which creates a DAG edge). Exactly one of the two per item.

entity.description: A sub-domain entity_types 'key' this capability needs (e.g. relationship_id). Satisfied from the blackboard's resolved entities — no upstream agent required.

from.description: A produced-output 'type' that some upstream capability declares in its io.produces. Matching this creates a producer→consumer edge in the DAG.

select.description: OPTIONAL, meaningful only alongside 'from': a CEL expression rooted at `input` (the producer's output) that projects/reshapes it into exactly what this consumer expects (field projection, not a blob pass-through). Absent = identity pass-through (today's behavior) — additive and backward-compatible. Evaluated by the gateway's Blackboard; matched/interpreted generically, never domain-hardcoded.

produces.description: Named, typed outputs this capability places on the blackboard for downstream steps to consume via 'from'.

name.description: Blackboard key under which this capability's result is published for downstream binding.

type.description: Symbolic output type used to match a downstream consumer's 'from'. Manifest-declared; matched by string equality.

entities.description: Manifest-declared entity ids emitted by this output. Runtime filtering uses these selectors only; the gateway never scans arbitrary JSON for ids.

type.description: Entity type whose coverage service is declared by the domain/sub-domain manifest.

select.description: CEL expression rooted at `output` (this producer output) that evaluates to a string id or array of string ids, e.g. output.items.map(x, x.id).

figures.description: Manifest-declared load-bearing figures emitted by this output. The gateway renders these from data and validates answer attribution generically.

label.description: Human label the answer must use when attributing this rendered figure.

path.description: CEL expression rooted at `output` (this producer output) that evaluates to the figure's raw scalar value; guard optional fields with has(...).

format.description: Generic formatter id. Enumerated to exactly the formats GroundedFigureRenderer renders and GroundedFigureValidator gates on; a value outside this set is rejected at ingestion instead of silently rendering as plain and weakening the grounding gate.

condition.description: OPTIONAL node-level CEL expression rooted at `input` (this node's merged, bound input). It must evaluate to boolean; true dispatches the node, false cleanly skips it as not applicable.

map.description: OPTIONAL dynamic map expansion. The gateway evaluates 'over' against this node's merged, bound input; each selected item is optionally reshaped by 'item_select' and dispatched to this same capability, bounded by the declared caps and global gateway ceilings.

over.description: CEL expression rooted at `input` (the bound input) that must evaluate to an array.

item_select.description: OPTIONAL CEL expression rooted at `item` (each array element) that produces the per-item wire input.

max_items.description: OPTIONAL per-node item cap; runtime clamps to the gateway global maximum.

max_concurrency.description: OPTIONAL per-node concurrency cap; runtime clamps to the gateway global maximum.

transport.description: MCP HTTP transport. 'streamable' = Streamable HTTP (single /mcp endpoint, current spec); 'sse' = legacy, deprecated HTTP+SSE two-channel handshake. Absent = streamable.

protocol_version.description: Optional per-agent MCP protocol version override negotiated on initialize. When absent, the gateway uses conduit.mcp.protocol-version (streamable) / conduit.mcp.legacy-protocol-version (sse).
